epsilon_greedy.comment: Epsilon-greedy with EWMA. reward_scale=200 puts values in [0.25,1.0] range. gamma=0.90 (alpha=0.10) detects DDoS/mobility changes in ~8 steps (~10s). epsilon=0.15 balances exploration vs exploitation in 151-step simulations.

ucb1.comment: UCB1 with EWMA. reward_scale=200 (=10000/50ms) puts values in [0.25,1.0]: bonus(0.32)<diff(0.75) after warmup so algorithm exploits correctly. gamma=0.90 gives alpha=0.10 for fast DDoS/mobility detection (~8 steps).

linucb.comment: LinUCB (Li et al. 2010) with arm-indicator features. reward_scale=100 normalizes feedback to ~[0.5,2.0] compatible with identity A. alpha=1.0 (standard UCB1 paper value) gives wider confidence interval for proper exploration. gamma=0.99 balances stability with non-stationary adaptation.

thompson_sampling.comment: Contextual Thompson Sampling. lr=0.5 converges fast in 151-step simulations. prior_precision=1.0 gives informative prior to avoid initial oscillation. alpha=1.0 reduces blind exploration. gamma=0.99 detects DDoS/mobility in ~7 steps.

ppo_hybrid.comment: Hybrid PPO. hidden_dim=32 gives ~1.5k params (right ratio for 151-step simulation vs 24k with dim=128). reward_scale=200 puts _last_reward_norm in [0.1,1.0] (full tanh range). batch_size=16 trains 2x more often. lr=0.001 for fast convergence.

sac_hybrid.comment: Hybrid SAC. hidden_dim=32 gives ~1.5k params for 151-step simulation. reward_scale=200 fixes _last_reward_norm saturation. tau=0.02 for faster target network adaptation. replay_size=512 starts training after 32 steps (valid for short simulations). lr=0.001 for fast convergence.

random.comment: Selects a random server at each step. Baseline for exploration-only behavior.

best.comment: Always selects the server with the lowest observed latency. Oracle upper-bound baseline.
